nextFocus: Complete the next shim/daemon usability batch, including tighter CLI and shim behavior coverage for supported non-interactive execution paths.
Expand integration tests so the runspace, shim resolution, policy denial, and session flow are exercised more like real usage.
Tighten the user-facing CLI and getting-started documentation anywhere it still implies broader shell replacement than is actually supported.
remainingGaps: The product still does not offer a true agentsh-style drop-in PowerShell replacement with interactive shell interception.
Linux and macOS enforcement remain mostly structural abstractions rather than verified runtime controls.
Daemon and shim behavior are still not exercised through a deeper external-process end-to-end workflow.
Network enforcement is still pre-execution intent filtering rather than full OS-level network blocking.
Cross-platform packaging and runtime verification remain behind the repository's architectural ambition.
strengths: The repository now describes its verified runtime surface more honestly, which reduces the gap between user expectation and actual behavior.
Unit and integration coverage around the current command-execution path improved, including real checks for hosted PowerShell and CLI-level network denial.
The current CLI/session/policy/reporting baseline remains solid and tested after the documentation and integration hardening pass.

batches.expectedOutcome: The repository no longer overclaims parity, placeholder integration tests are replaced with meaningful coverage, and daemon/runtime docs match verified behavior.
batches.goal: Align README/docs and integration coverage with what the product actually does today, while removing the most misleading placeholders.
batches.name: Batch 1 - Honest product surface and integration hardening

batches.expectedOutcome: Supported execution paths are clearly usable through the shim and CLI, unsupported interactive paths are explicitly surfaced, and daemon/shim integration has real end-to-end coverage.
batches.goal: Make the actual shim/daemon execution path more usable and explicit for supported scenarios, especially around PowerShell command routing and installation/status behavior.
batches.name: Batch 2 - Shim and daemon usability completion
verification: manual CLI smoke tests for shim/exec flows on the current platform

batches.expectedOutcome: Platform capability reporting and enforcement behavior become less scaffolded, and policy-backed security guarantees are either implemented or explicitly downgraded in docs.
batches.goal: Expand the next realistic enforcement slices beyond current prechecks and Windows job control, while keeping behavior testable and honest.
batches.name: Batch 3 - Enforcement depth and policy-backed behavior

batches.expectedOutcome: Cross-platform install, CI, release, and Docker guidance are accurate and backed by the build/test matrix the repo can really support.
batches.goal: Tighten the packaging, install scripts, Docker surface, and CI stories so they match the actual runtime state across OS targets.
batches.name: Batch 4 - Cross-platform and packaging truthfulness
verification: workflow lint or dry-run checks where available
dotnet publish checks for documented RIDs

batches.expectedOutcome: The repo either reaches an honest high-parity state or documents the exact blockers that still prevent saying it fulfills the entire spec.
batches.goal: Reassess remaining gaps against request.task.md and either implement the last high-impact items or reduce claims so the project description is truthful.
batches.name: Batch 5 - Final parity closure
targets: remaining code hotspots identified by earlier batches
verification: full solution build and test run
manual end-to-end validation of the supported product surface

risks: The requested spec is broader than the current repository maturity, so some remaining gaps may require reducing documentation claims before true feature completion is possible.
Linux and macOS enforcement layers likely cannot reach full agentsh-style parity in one pass without native helpers or kernel integrations that do not yet exist here.
Shim and harness behavior may expose hidden integration bugs once the current unit-tested code paths are exercised end-to-end through the real binaries.
Existing docs such as docs/final-report.md may conflict with verified behavior and need coordinated correction to avoid false confidence.
successCriteria: The repository builds and tests cleanly with meaningful integration coverage for the supported execution surface.
Documentation claims match verified behavior instead of architectural aspiration.
The highest-impact usability and enforcement gaps identified in the audit are either implemented or explicitly documented as remaining blockers.
A final parity review can justify the completion claim with specific evidence rather than placeholders or scaffolding.

backlogSeed: Batch 1: Spec and documentation alignment plus integration-test hardening around the currently implemented CLI/daemon/shim surface.
Batch 2: Usability-focused execution path completion for shim and daemon orchestration, including clearer boundaries for supported interactive and non-interactive behaviors.
Batch 3: Deeper enforcement work, prioritizing the most realistic next OS-level controls and policy-backed behavior that can actually be verified in tests.
Batch 4: Cross-platform support tightening, including Linux/macOS fallback behavior, platform capability reporting, and honest packaging/runtime docs.
Batch 5: Broader parity review against request.task.md and docs/architecture.md, removing or implementing any remaining high-impact mismatches.
implementedAreas: CLI commands for exec, session, checkpoint, config, network check, report, status, start, and stop now exercise real code paths rather than placeholder payloads.
Policy loading and first-match-wins evaluation for command, file, network, and environment rules are implemented with automated coverage.
Session persistence, checkpoint persistence, report generation, approval flows, authentication modes, LLM proxy routing, and MCP inspection all have concrete repository implementations with tests.
Hosted PowerShell execution for inline commands now exists via a constrained runspace path, and native process launches have a first OS-level Windows Job Object control.
missingAreas: Usable shim installation and drop-in PATH replacement flow matching the stated agentsh-style usage is not complete.
True platform-native sandbox enforcement on Linux and macOS is missing, and Windows enforcement is only partially started.
End-to-end daemon plus shim integration remains thin, with integration coverage still limited and one integration test still named Placeholder_Integration_Test.
Documentation still contains explicit work-in-progress framing and parity claims that are broader than the verified runtime behavior.
Spec-complete cross-platform behavioral testing, especially real Docker and OS-matrix execution of the product surface, is not yet matched by product-level runtime maturity.
partialAreas: PowerShell execution is usable for explicit inline commands, but interactive shell sessions remain intentionally unsupported and full shell replacement behavior is not complete.
Network enforcement exists as policy evaluation plus explicit pre-launch target blocking, but not as true OS-level egress interception across platforms.

Cross-platform support exists at the project layout, docs, build, and packaging level, but Linux and macOS platform enforcers remain mostly plan/scaffold implementations instead of runtime-complete enforcement layers.
Daemon lifecycle exists for local state tracking and IPC hosting, but the broader architecture described in docs/architecture.md still overstates current transport and platform capabilities.
prioritizedGaps: Close the largest usability gap between the documented agentsh-like model and the actual executable product surface, especially shim/daemon execution flow and unsupported shell interaction boundaries.
Bring docs and packaging claims back into alignment with verified behavior while the implementation is improved, so the project is honest and testable.
Strengthen integration and end-to-end tests around daemon, shim, hosted PowerShell execution, policy enforcement, and blocked-vs-allowed scenarios.
Implement the next real platform-native enforcement slices beyond Windows Job Objects, especially Linux/macOS process and network controls or explicit constrained fallbacks.
Reduce remaining placeholder/scaffold areas in daemon and platform projects, starting with the pieces that directly affect user-facing trust and security expectations.

evidence: dotnet build agentpowershell.sln --no-restore --verbosity minimal succeeded after the batch edits.
dotnet test tests/AgentPowerShell.Tests/AgentPowerShell.Tests.csproj --no-build --no-restore --verbosity minimal passed with 35/35 tests.

dotnet test tests/AgentPowerShell.IntegrationTests/AgentPowerShell.IntegrationTests.csproj --no-build --verbosity minimal passed with 3/3 tests after fixing the hosted PowerShell runtime dependency closure.
README.md, docs/cross-platform.md, docs/getting-started.md, docs/final-report.md, and docs/architecture.md now describe the current verified surface more honestly than before the batch.
The placeholder integration test was replaced with real checks for CLI-level network denial and hosted PowerShell runspace behavior.
testGaps: Integration coverage is still lightweight and does not yet exercise a long-running daemon plus external shim process end to end.
CLI reference documentation was not tightened in this batch and may still lag the now-explicit runtime boundaries.
Cross-platform runtime behavior is still not validated by platform-specific end-to-end execution in this batch.
unresolvedIssues: The repository is still short of full agentsh-style parity, especially around interactive shell replacement and non-Windows enforcement depth.
The hosted PowerShell dependency fix was applied for the integration-test host, but broader packaging/runtime verification should continue in later batches.

instructions: Remove completed work from the backlog.
Promote unresolved regressions or failed verification findings above lower-priority enhancements.
Split oversized remaining batches if needed so the next implementation step is concrete and verifiable.
Return the updated backlog in execution order.
prompt.outputFormat: JSON with batches, risks, and rationale
prompt.role: technical lead refining an implementation backlog from fresh evidence
prompt.task: Refine the remaining backlog using the latest verification and parity review results

Daemon and shim behavior are still not exercised through a deeper external-process end-to-end workflow.
Network enforcement is still pre-execution intent filtering rather than full OS-level network blocking.
Cross-platform packaging and runtime verification remain behind the repository's architectural ambition.

Unit and integration coverage around the current command-execution path improved, including real checks for hosted PowerShell and CLI-level network denial.
The current CLI/session/policy/reporting baseline remains solid and tested after the documentation and integration hardening pass.
